title: Tapping to Build Daily Calm Grounding
subtitle: A daytime tapping practice for training the nervous system to return to calm more quickly
excerpt: A five-minute daytime tapping practice from Day 25 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The script addresses the pattern of running on stress — where calm is the exception rather than the trained default. The reframe moves toward calm as a practised, repeatable state that the body learns to find on its own.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 25 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

practice.whenToUse: Use in the daytime when you notice stress has been running the day, or when you want to train a faster return to calm before the tension carries into the night.

content.text: A five-minute daytime tapping practice from Day 25 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The script targets the running-on-stress pattern: where the default mode is urgency and calm is something that requires effort rather than something the body finds on its own. The reframe moves toward calm as a practised default — something that builds through repetition.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I used to live by stress alone, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though calm didn't come naturally, I honour the practice it takes to build it.
Even though I forget sometimes, I am open to making calm my automatic state.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: Calm isn't luck — it's repetition.
Side of eye: I can train my system to return there faster.
Under eye: One deep breath is a step in that direction.
Under nose: One pause between tasks counts too.
Chin: My body learns from what I repeat.
Collarbone: I am teaching it peace on purpose.
Under arm: Practice builds stability.
Top of head: I am creating a new normal through small moments.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: Calm is my default setting now.
Side of eye: I return to it more easily each day.
Under eye: My body trusts this state.
Under nose: I keep choosing softness over speed.
Chin: Consistency becomes comfort.
Collarbone: I am proud of the peace I am building.
Under arm: I make calm my daily practice.
Top of head: I live from quiet strength.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 25 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
